content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 23, 1988

Randy Unruh
4211 Duquesne Ave., #4
Culver City, CA 90232

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-88-088

Dear Mr. Unruh:

You have asked for advice concerning the campaign disclosure
provisions of the Political Reform Act.1/

QUESTION

May the Friends of Randy Unruh Committee receive a
contribution from the Friends of Jess Unruh Committee in an amount
up to $90,000?

CONCLUSION

The Act does not prohibit a candidate or committee from making
contributions to or receiving contributions from another candidate
or committee nor does it limit the amount of such contributions.
However, you should check with the filing officer of the
jurisdiction for which you are a candidate to determine if there
is a local ordinance which prohibits or places limits on
contributions.

FACTS

You are a candidate in an April 12, 1988 election. You are
seeking a contribution from the Friends of Jess Unruh up to
$90,000.

1/Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Randy Unruh
Page Two

ANALYSIS

The Act requires candidates and campaign committees to file
periodic reports disclosing money raised and spent in connection
with elections. (Sections 84200-84221.) The Act contains no
provisions prohibiting or limiting contributions from one
candidate or committee to another candidate or committee.
However, some cities and counties have adopted ordinances which
prohibit or limit contributions received by candidates running for
offices in their jurisdictions. Therefore, you also should check
with the local filing officer to determine if you may receive a
$90,000 contribution from the Friends of Jess Unruh Committee.

If you have any further questions, please do not hesitate to
contact me at (916) 322-5662.

Sincerely,

Diane M. Griffiths
General Counsel

[Signature: Carla Wardlow]

By: Carla Wardlow
    Political Reform Consultant

February 24, 1988

Randy Unruh
Friends of Randy Unruh (#880227)
4211 Duquesne Ave. #4
Culver City, CA 90232
(213) 280-0670

General Counsel
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Ste. 800
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Sir,

I need a written opinion from your office regarding whether it
would be legal and acceptable under California law for my
campaign committee to receive a contribution from Friends of Jess
Unruh up to the amount of $90,000.

I would appreciate it if you could expedite this matter as my
election date is April Twelfth and I am seeking this contribution
by March Fourth.

Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.

Sincerely,

Randy Unruh

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission
February 29, 1988


Randy Unruh
Friends of Randy Unruh
4211 Duquesne Avenue, #4
Culver City, CA 90232

Re: 88-088

Dear Mr. Unruh:

Your letter requesting advice under the Political Reform
Act was received on February 26, 1988 by the Fair Political
Practices Commission. If you have any questions about your
advice request, you may contact me directly at (916) 322-5662.

We try to answer all advice requests promptly. Therefore,
unless your request poses particularly complex legal questions,
or more information is needed, you should expect a response
within 21 working days if your request seeks formal written
advice. If more information is needed, the person assigned to
prepare a response to your request will contact you shortly to
advise you as to the information needed. If your request is
for informal assistance, we will answer it as quickly as we
can. (See Commission Regulation 18329 (2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Sec. 18329).)

You also should be aware that your letter and our response
are public records which may be disclosed to the public upon
receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Very truly yours,

[illegible signature]
Jeanne Pritchard
Chief
Technical Assistance and Analysis
Division
